nodes.summary: Top-level training orchestrator for a Maxent run.
nodes.description: Loads occurrence and background data, applies CV/bootstrap/subsample replication, builds base features from grids via Extractor, expands them into linear/quadratic/product/threshold/hinge features, runs Sequential to optimise lambdas, then writes the .lambdas file, projects predictions, computes AUC, runs jackknife and permutation-importance analyses, and emits per-species HTML reports.

methods.description: Entry point. Parses params, initialises grids via initializeGrids(), splits samples for replicates, then for each species calls maxentRun() and writes outputs.
Builds an Extractor (or GridSetFromFile for SWD) that reads environmental layers, training samples and test samples, plus optional bias grid. Returns the populated GridSet.

methods.signature: MaxentRunResults maxentRun(Feature[] features, Sample[] ss, Sample[] testSamples)
methods.description: Constructs a FeaturedSpace from features and samples, hands it to Sequential.run() which iteratively updates lambdas, returns gain + iterations + FeaturedSpace.

From raw layer features, builds the full feature set: binary expansions of categorical layers, linear/quadratic/product/threshold/hinge generators on continuous ones, with naturalClamp on linear features.

methods.signature: double[][] jackknifeGain(Feature[] baseFeatures, Sample[] ss, Sample[] testSamples, double allGain, double allTestGain, double allauc)
methods.description: For each predictor: trains a model with it omitted and one with it alone; records training gain, test gain and test AUC for the jackknife plots that quantify variable importance.

methods.signature: double[][] writeCumulativeIndex(double[] weights, String raw2cumfile, FeaturedSpace X, double auc, double trainauc, Feature[] baseFeatures, double entropy)
methods.description: Sorts the background raw outputs and accumulates the cumulative distribution. Writes the omission file giving (raw, cumulative, occurrence-probability, area, train-omission, test-omission) for every threshold, and computes 9–11 threshold rules (fixed cumulative, equal sensitivity/specificity, max sens+spec, etc).

methods.signature: static double[][] responsePlotData(Grid projgrid, HashMap map, String var, double min, double max, double[] categories, boolean exponent)
methods.description: Computes a 1-D response curve: holds all other variables at their sample-mean and sweeps one variable across its range, querying the projected grid at each sample value.

methods.signature: void createProfiles(Feature[] baseFeatures, String lambdafile, Sample[] samples)
methods.description: Builds and writes response-curve PNGs and per-variable .dat files via ResponsePlot, using the mean sample values as the holding context.
Writes the percent-contribution table comparing gain-based contributions to PermutationImportance results into the HTML and maxentResults.csv.
Closes the results CSV, the HTML, the log, and the progress monitor.

nodes.summary: Core L1-regularised maximum-entropy optimizer.
nodes.description: Implements the sequential coordinate-descent / Newton optimisation of the Maxent log-likelihood with L1 regularisation. Each iteration picks the feature with the largest delta-loss bound, computes a Newton step (or a search step for non-binary features), and updates lambdas, density and linearPredictor. Periodically performs a parallel Newton update over recently changed features. Stops when per-iteration loss drop falls below convergenceThreshold or after maxIterations.

methods.description: Main loop. For iter = 0..maxIterations: either calls doParallelUpdate (every parallelUpdateFrequency iters) or picks getBestFeature() and calls doSequentialUpdate; tests termination via terminationTest. Returns final regularised loss.
Scans all active features and all FeatureGenerators, returning the one whose deltaLossBound(h) is most negative — i.e. the feature predicted to reduce loss the most for an optimal step.

Computes the closed-form upper bound on loss reduction achievable by adding alpha*h: -N1*alpha + log(W0 + W1*exp(alpha)) + beta1*(|lambda+alpha|-|lambda|), evaluated at the analytic optimal alpha returned by goodAlpha.
Closed-form optimum step for a single binary-style coordinate: alpha = log((N1-beta1)*W0 / ((N0+beta1)*W1)) (or its sign-flipped variant), clipped so it doesn't cross zero into the wrong L1 quadrant.
Newton step in direction of unit vector at h: stepSize = -deriv(h) / uTHu, where uTHu is the variance of h under the current density. Clamped so lambda does not change sign.

Newton step in an arbitrary direction u over the active features: computes the model-density-weighted u^T H u and uses stepSize = -deriv·u / u^T H u, clipped per-coordinate at the L1 zero crossings. Used by doParallelUpdate.

Builds the direction u as the previous-iteration lambda increments, takes a Newton step in that direction over all features, and reverts if loss did not decrease. Attributes the per-feature contribution share proportional to recent deltaContribution.

Performs the per-feature update: for binary features uses goodAlpha; for continuous uses newtonStep with line-search fallback if the delta-loss bound is violated, then increaseLambda + setDensity update. Bookkeeps h.contribution.
Backtracking search: doubles alpha while newLossChange keeps decreasing, then takes one extra factor-2 step if it still improves. Used when Newton's prediction overshoots the L1-regularised loss.

methods.signature: double newLossChange(Feature h, double alpha, int square)
methods.description: Evaluates the exact one-step change in loss if h.lambda += alpha: -alpha*N1 + log(sum density*exp(alpha*h)) + beta*(|lambda+alpha|-|lambda|).
Sub-gradient of regularised loss with respect to h.lambda: (W1 - N1) ± beta, with the sign chosen by current sign(lambda) or the active sub-gradient at zero.
Selects which features get their expectation refreshed this iteration: recently-changed features, the round-robin slice, and the top-K by deltaLossBound.
Every convergenceTestFrequency=20 iterations, stops when (previousLoss - newLoss) < terminationThreshold, or if gain exceeds 10000 (numerical safety).

nodes.summary: Holds features, lambdas, linear predictor and density over background points.
nodes.description: The state object for the Maxent model. Maintains linearPredictor[i] = Σⱼ λⱼ·fⱼ(i), the linearPredictorNormalizer = max_i linearPredictor (for numerical safety), density[i] = bias(i)·exp(linearPredictor[i] − normalizer), and densityNormalizer = Σ density[i]. Computes feature sample expectations with sub-sampling deviations β, handles bias-as-prior, and provides AUC, entropy, prevalence, training/test loss.

methods.signature: FeaturedSpace(Sample[] samples, Feature[] f, Params params)
methods.description: Builds the space from features and samples. Sorts features into linear/binary/threshold-generator/hinge-generator/bias buckets, initialises bias-divisor and bias-distribution, then sets sample expectations and the initial linearPredictor/density arrays.

methods.signature: FeaturedSpace(GridSet gs, String lambdaFile, boolean clamp, boolean renormalize)
methods.description: Reconstructs a trained model from a .lambdas file: parses each feature description (product, square, polyhedral, binary '=', threshold '<', hinge '\'' or '`', or linear), looks up the underlying base Feature in the GridSet, applies ClampedFeature wrappers and recomputes the density.

methods.signature: void increaseLambda(Feature h, double alpha, Feature[] updateFeatures)
methods.description: Increments h.lambda by alpha, updates linearPredictor[i] += alpha·h.eval(i) for all i, recomputes linearPredictorNormalizer = max linearPredictor, then setDensity() refreshes density and the expectations of updateFeatures.

Recomputes density[i] = biasDist(i)·exp(linearPredictor[i] − normalizer), accumulates densityNormalizer = Σ density[i], and for each f in toUpdate sets f.expectation = (Σ density[i]·f.eval(i)) / densityNormalizer.
Recomputes the linear predictor from scratch: linearPredictor[i] = Σⱼ λⱼ·fⱼ.eval(i), then calls setLinearPredictorNormalizer to capture the max.

For each feature f, computes (avg, std, min, max) of f/biasDiv at the samples, then sets f.sampleExpectation = midpoint and f.sampleDeviation = beta·std/√m (the half-width of the confidence interval for the empirical mean), with floor minDeviation. Inactivates features with 0 sample data.
Computes the area-under-ROC of model density against the given samples vs the background distribution. Uses the DeLong et al. 1988 unbiased estimator (their equation 2) to also compute aucSD.
Computes − Σᵢ pᵢ log pᵢ where pᵢ = density[i]/densityNormalizer, the entropy of the fitted distribution used to convert raw → logistic/cloglog.
Returns the average occurrence probability over background points: (1/N) Σ τ(density[i]/Z, entropy), with τ either logistic or cloglog.
Returns −Σⱼ λⱼ·π̃[fⱼ] + linearPredictorNormalizer + log(densityNormalizer), the regularised maximum-likelihood objective Sequential is minimising.
Returns Σⱼ |λⱼ|·βⱼ, the L1 regularisation penalty.
Writes the .lambdas file: one line per feature with name, lambda, min, max — plus linearPredictorNormalizer, densityNormalizer, numBackgroundPoints, entropy.

nodes.summary: Base class for threshold/hinge knot placement — sorts background+samples by feature value.
nodes.description: Pre-sorts the union of background points and samples by feature value, collapses near-duplicates within a precision bound to give a set of candidate threshold positions thr[]. Subclasses ThrFeatureGenerator and HingeFeatureGenerator use this index to compute sample expectations and feature expectations for every candidate threshold in linear-in-N passes.

methods.description: Caches feature values for all background points and samples, sorts the combined index by feature value, walks the sorted list to find distinct thresholds (separated by at least the auto-detected numerical precision), and records the mapping val↔threshold.

methods.signature: abstract double featureEval(int thrIndex, int i)
methods.description: Evaluates the synthetic feature at threshold thrIndex at background point i — overridden by Thr/Hinge generators.
Returns an on-demand Feature wrapper backed by this generator at the given threshold, sharing lambda/expectation bookkeeping with the generator.

Returns the smallest power-of-ten threshold-spacing required to distinguish f from a nearby value, by walking up to maxDigits significant digits and looking for 00/01/98/99 trailing pairs (floating-point rounding artefacts).

nodes.summary: Generates hinge features (x − knot)+/(max − knot) at every candidate knot.
nodes.description: For every candidate threshold thr[i], the hinge feature is max(0, (x − thr[i])/(maxVal − thr[i])). Computes sample expectations and feature expectations in a single sorted-pass: maintains running sums Σ x·w and Σ w over the right tail, from which the conditional mean and variance of x − thr above each threshold follow in O(1) per threshold.

methods.description: Walks the sorted index right-to-left accumulating Σ x/biasDiv and Σ (x/biasDiv)² over samples. At each threshold computes the hinge sample-mean = ((sum1 − thr·wsum1) / (maxVal − thr)) / m and standard deviation, builds an Interval (avg ± β·std/√m) and stores its midpoint and half-width as f.sampleExpectation and f.sampleDeviation.
Walks sorted points right-to-left accumulating Σ x·density and Σ density; at each threshold the hinge feature expectation is (sum − thr·wsum) / densityNormalizer / (maxVal − thr).
Materialises a concrete HingeFeature(feature, thr[thrIndex], maxVal) when Sequential picks this threshold, copying sampleExpectation, sampleDeviation, expectation and beta from the generator.

nodes.summary: Generates threshold features 𝟙[x > knot] at every candidate knot.
nodes.description: Maintains running sums and squared-sums of 1/biasDiv over the samples seen so far (sorted by feature value); converts these into mean and standard deviation of the threshold's indicator under the empirical distribution, then β-scaled confidence interval to set the sample expectation and deviation.

methods.description: Sweep right-to-left over sortedVals; whenever a sample is encountered, accumulate val = 1/biasDiv(sample) into sum1 and sum1². At each threshold compute avg = sum1/m, std = √((sum2 − m·avg²)/(m−1)), capped to thrInfo.max/2, then sampleExpectation = mid of Interval(avg±β/√m·std).
Accumulates Σ density along the sorted order; the threshold feature expectation at thr[i] is the cumulative tail mass / densityNormalizer.
Materialises a ThresholdFeature(feature, thr[thrIndex], name) and copies sampleExpectation/Deviation/expectation/beta from the generator.

nodes.summary: Interface for lazy feature families with a continuum of candidates.
nodes.description: Abstract contract for feature generators that expose many candidate features without materialising them all. Provides per-index access, sample/feature expectation updates, and a way to export the chosen feature when the Sequential optimizer picks one.

methods.description: Compute and store each candidate's empirical mean and beta-scaled deviation under the current bias.
Refresh each candidate's expectation under the current model density.
Materialise a permanent Feature for the chosen candidate.
Lightweight on-demand view of a candidate (used by Sequential.deltaLossBound).

nodes.summary: Abstract base class for all Maxent features.
nodes.description: Defines the type-tag enumeration (BINARY, LINEAR, SQUARE, PRODUCT, THR_GEN, THR, L_CONT, L_CAT, L_F_BIAS_OUT, L_DEBIAS_AVG, F_W_SAMPLES, HINGE_GEN, HINGE), bookkeeping fields (lambda, sampleExpectation, sampleDeviation, expectation, contribution, beta, active), and abstract eval(int) / eval(Sample) methods. Subclasses provide the specific functional form.

methods.description: Evaluate the feature at background point index p.
Evaluate the feature at a presence sample.
Returns the integer feature-type tag by unwrapping caching/scaling/clamping wrappers and dispatching on the inner concrete class.
Returns the sorted array of distinct values the feature takes across the background — used by BinaryFeature.makeAll to expand categorical layers.
Tests whether the feature is constant across all background points (treated as a mask layer).

nodes.summary: Linear feature: f(x) = inner feature's value.
nodes.description: Trivial pass-through wrapper used to make explicit the linear term over a base layer feature so that .lambdas files record the linear coefficient explicitly even when its value is 0 (needed for clamping at apply-time).

methods.description: Returns f.eval(p) where f is the wrapped continuous base feature.
Returns f.eval(s) at a sample.

nodes.summary: Linearly rescales a feature to [0, 1] using (val − min) / (max − min).
nodes.description: Stores the min/max range observed in the training set and exposes the linearly rescaled value. Maxent stores all features in a comparable [0,1] scale so that β regularisation parameters are comparable across feature types.

methods.description: Returns (f.eval(p) − min) / scale, where scale = max − min.
Returns (f.eval(s) − min) / scale.

nodes.summary: Pre-computes and caches feature values over all background points.
nodes.description: Materialises the n-vector of f.eval(0..n-1) once at construction, then serves O(1) lookups. Samples are still evaluated through the wrapped feature.

methods.description: Returns cached vals[p].

nodes.summary: ScaledFeature with cached values across the background.
nodes.description: Combines ScaledFeature with CachedFeature so that the rescaled (val − min)/scale values are computed once and stored.

methods.description: Returns the cached rescaled value vals[p].

nodes.summary: Clamps a feature's value to [min, max] during projection.
nodes.description: Used at apply-time so the model does not extrapolate when a projection layer's value at a new site lies outside the training range. Returns min/max if the value is out of range, otherwise the original value.

methods.description: Returns val clipped to [min, max].
Same clip at a sample.

nodes.summary: A feature that returns a constant c at every point.
nodes.description: Used as the default bias (1.0) and as a placeholder for bias-as-prior when no bias is supplied.

methods.description: Returns the constant c.

nodes.summary: Hinge: f(x) = max(0, (x − min)/(max − min)).
nodes.description: A one-sided piecewise-linear feature: 0 below the knot, linearly increasing to 1 at max. Together with its reverse counterpart, hinges give Maxent the flexibility of GAM-style smooth responses without overfitting.

methods.description: Returns (d − min)/(max − min) if d > min, else 0.
Hinge of f.eval(i).
Hinge of f.eval(s).

nodes.summary: Marker subclass that flags a feature as a hinge-knot generator source.
nodes.description: Wraps a continuous base feature so makeFeatures can route it into a HingeFeatureGenerator instead of treating it as a linear feature. Eval methods pass through unchanged.

methods.description: Pass-through to underlying feature.

nodes.summary: Threshold: f(x) = 𝟙[x > threshold].
nodes.description: A binary step function at a given threshold knot. Used by Maxent's threshold-feature class to model abrupt changes in suitability.

methods.description: Returns 1.0 if f.eval(i) > threshold else 0.0.
Returns 1.0 if f.eval(s) > threshold else 0.0.

nodes.summary: Marker subclass identifying a base feature as a threshold-knot generator source.
nodes.description: Lets makeFeatures route this feature into a ThrFeatureGenerator. Eval methods pass through to the underlying continuous feature.

methods.description: Pass-through to wrapped feature.

nodes.summary: Product feature: f(x) = f1(x) · f2(x).
nodes.description: Captures pairwise interactions between two continuous (rescaled) base features.

methods.description: Returns f1.eval(p) * f2.eval(p).
Returns f1.eval(s) * f2.eval(s).

nodes.summary: Quadratic feature: f(x) = inner(x)².
nodes.description: Squared transform of a base feature. Together with the linear feature, lets Maxent fit unimodal responses.

methods.description: Returns f.eval(p)².
Returns f.eval(s)².

nodes.summary: Polyhedral feature: f(x) = (x − x²)/4.
nodes.description: Hidden experimental feature class; symmetric tent that peaks at x = 0.5. Used only when params.polyhedral is enabled.

methods.description: Returns (val − val²)/4.

nodes.summary: Binary indicator feature for categorical layers: f(x) = 𝟙[x == target].
nodes.description: One-hot expansion of a categorical layer. makeAll() enumerates all distinct values in the base feature and creates a BinaryFeature for each, marking the first as isFirstCategory.

methods.description: Returns 1.0 if f.eval(i) == target else 0.0.

methods.signature: static BinaryFeature[] makeAll(Feature f, String s)
methods.description: Returns one BinaryFeature per distinct value of f, expanding a categorical predictor into its one-hot indicator set.

nodes.summary: Feature whose background and sample values are stored as float arrays.
nodes.description: Used when background data and sample data are read out of SWD CSV files rather than from grids. Stores parallel backgroundVals[] and sampleVals[] indexed by point.

methods.description: Returns backgroundVals[p].
Returns sampleVals[s.getPoint()].

nodes.summary: Concatenates samples into the end of a feature's background point list.
nodes.description: Used when addSamplesToBackground is true: the n-th background point is the original feature value for p<f.n, or the sample at index p − f.n otherwise.

methods.description: Returns f.eval(p) if p<f.n else f.eval(ss[p − f.n]).

nodes.summary: Abstract feature backed by an environmental Layer, tagged with its Layer type.
nodes.description: Distinguishes continuous, categorical, mask, F_BIAS_OUT, DEBIAS_AVG, etc layers, so Feature.type() can dispatch correctly. Subclassed by Extractor and GridSetFromFile.

methods.description: Returns Layer.CONT / CAT / F_BIAS_OUT / DEBIAS_AVG / MASK / PROB / CUM.

nodes.summary: Abstract 2D raster of environmental values with type-tagged storage.
nodes.description: Defines the abstract eval(r,c) and hasData(r,c), plus run-length-encoded compressed-row factory vals2Grid for short/byte/int/float/double rasters with optional ubyte support. Provides histograms, bilinear interpolation, min/max, sum, masked traversal.

methods.signature: abstract float eval(int r, int c)
methods.description: Returns the (possibly type-cast) value of the grid at row r, column c.

methods.signature: abstract boolean hasData(int r, int c)
methods.description: Returns true iff (r,c) is in bounds and not equal to the NODATA value.
Single-pass scan over data cells returning [min, max].

methods.signature: double[] histogram(double min, double max, int n)
methods.description: Returns a normalised count histogram of the grid's values, binned into n equal-width bins between min and max.

methods.signature: double interpolate(double lon, double lat, float target, boolean match)
methods.description: Bilinear interpolation at geographic (lon,lat). If match=true, treats each pixel value as 0/1 indicator of equality to target, returning a fractional match strength.

methods.signature: static Grid vals2Grid(GridDimension dim, float[][] vals, String name, double NODATA, boolean compress)
methods.description: Factory: wraps a 2D float array into a Grid, optionally using run-length encoding row-by-row when compressionRatio<0.9 (Grid.compressedGrid).
Reinterprets signed bytes as unsigned (adds 256 to negatives) for grids that exceed signed-byte range.

nodes.summary: Grid backed by signed byte cells.
nodes.description: Concrete grid for predictor layers with small integer range; eval returns the byte cast to float.

methods.description: Byte-level accessor.

nodes.summary: Grid backed by 16-bit short cells.
nodes.description: Concrete grid for medium-range integer layers.

methods.description: Short accessor.

nodes.summary: Grid backed by 32-bit int cells.
nodes.description: Concrete grid for full-range integer layers.

methods.description: Int accessor.

nodes.summary: Grid backed by 64-bit double cells.
nodes.description: Concrete grid for high-precision floating-point layers.

methods.description: Double accessor.

nodes.summary: Grid backed by unsigned-byte (0..255) cells stored as shorts.
nodes.description: Concrete grid for unsigned 8-bit layers.

methods.description: Unsigned-byte accessor returning 0..255.

nodes.summary: Geographic dimension descriptor (xllcorner, yllcorner, cellsize, nrows, ncols).
nodes.description: Immutable header describing a raster's geometry. Translates between (row,col) pixel coords and (x,y) geographic coords, with toRow/toCol/toX/toY/inBounds helpers.

methods.description: Geographic latitude → row index.
Geographic longitude → column index.
Column index → cell-centre longitude.
Row index → cell-centre latitude.
Header equality used to verify that all environmental layers share the same geometry.

nodes.summary: Reads .asc, .mxe, .grd/.gri and .bil rasters into Grid objects.
nodes.description: Dispatches on file extension: ASCII Esri grid (.asc), Maxent compressed binary (.mxe), Diva-GIS / generic (.grd + .gri) and BIL (.bil + .hdr). Detects native datatype (byte/short/int/float/double/ubyte), optionally shrinks oversized grids via stride sampling, and run-length-compresses rows.

methods.description: Picks readASC/readMXE/readGRD based on suffix and returns a typed, optionally compressed Grid.
Parses an ESRI ASCII grid header (ncols, nrows, xll, yll, cellsize, NODATA_value) then streams the value matrix, auto-detecting short vs float; downcasts to byte if min/max fits.
Reads the gzipped binary Maxent format: dimension, NODATA_value, nativeType, then a typed matrix.
Parses the .grd INI-style header (or .hdr for BIL) and streams the little-endian .gri/.bil binary into a Grid.

nodes.summary: Writes Grid objects out as .asc / .mxe / .grd-.gri / .bil / .csv.
nodes.description: Streaming writer: chooses the right binary or text format based on the output suffix, tracks min/max during writing for the .grd header, handles UBYTE→short widening on .grd output (Diva 5.2 bug workaround), and supports batched multi-grid writes for replicate summaries.

methods.description: Walks the grid row by row writing every cell to the chosen format.

methods.signature: static void writeGrids(Grid[] grids, String[] filenames, boolean notscientific)
methods.description: Writes several aligned grids in lock-step (used by AvgStderr for avg/std/min/max/median/lowerci summaries).

nodes.summary: Pan/zoomable Swing canvas for browsing a Grid with a heatmap color ramp.
nodes.description: Subclass of Zoom that maps grid values via a red-yellow-green-blue scale based on per-grid min/max.

methods.description: Switches the displayed raster and recomputes the colour scale.
Returns the pixel RGB for grid cell (r,c) by linear interpolation in HSV-ish ramp.

nodes.summary: Wraps a LazyGrid and exposes a downsampled view by keeping every k-th cell.
nodes.description: Used when GridIO.maxRowsAndCols caps the raster: subsamples on read to bound memory.

methods.description: Returns grid.eval(r·k, c·k).

nodes.summary: Streams Grid contents row-at-a-time without materialising the full matrix.
nodes.description: Memory-friendly grid that opens the underlying file lazily and advances forward when eval(r,c) is called. Throws if asked for a row earlier than the current cursor. Used heavily by Extractor and Project so they can hold many predictor layers in memory simultaneously.

methods.description: Opens the file (.asc / .mxe / .grd / .bil) and reads the header.
Advances row-by-row until row r, then returns currentRow[c].

nodes.summary: Holds a collection of aligned grids and exposes them as Features.
nodes.description: Parent class for Extractor and GridSetFromFile. Stores the layers, the shared GridDimension, train and test SampleSet2s, and a name→Feature map.

methods.description: Returns one LayerFeature per environmental layer, lazily built.
Looks up a feature by layer name, fatal-error if absent.

nodes.summary: Stub GridSet that creates features on-demand from sample featureMaps.
nodes.description: Used when applying a trained model to samples that already carry per-feature values in their HashMap (no background grids needed). Each feature evaluation simply pulls from sample.featureMap.

methods.description: Returns (or lazily creates) a Feature whose eval(Sample) reads s.featureMap.get(name).

nodes.summary: Reads a SWD-format CSV (one row per background point with all env values) as a GridSet.
nodes.description: Used as an alternative to per-layer raster directories. Each row is a background point with x,y plus one column per environmental variable; the resulting 'grid' is a 1×N strip, and feature.eval(p) reads allvals[p][i].

methods.description: Parses the SWD CSV, verifies all requested layers are present, and stores allvals[p][i].
Returns a 1-row virtual Grid backed by the column for `name`.

nodes.summary: Tags an environmental variable with its name and semantic type.
nodes.description: Constant integers for Continuous/Categorical/F_BIAS_OUT/Mask/Probability/Cumulative/DebiasAvg drive how the layer is converted to features by Runner.makeFeatures.

methods.description: Returns the type constant.
Returns the layer name.

nodes.summary: Reads grids + samples and extracts background and sample feature values.
nodes.description: The primary GridSet implementation. Opens each environmental layer as a LazyGrid, scans the rasters once to (a) record sample env-values at each sample's pixel, (b) reservoir-sample up to maxBackground background points where all layers have data. Caches each ASCII layer into a .mxe file for faster re-reads.

methods.signature: void extractSamples(String[] files, int nRandom, String trainsamples, String testsamples, Layer[] layers, String[] species)
methods.description: Top-level extraction: opens grids, parses train/test SampleSet2s, calls readSamples to pull background and sample values into Rc records.

Single pass over rows of the rasters: at each sample's (r,c) writes env values into the sample's data array; at every other cell reservoir-samples into randextract[] until n background points are collected.

methods.signature: static String CachedGridFilename(String filename, boolean makeit)
methods.description: Converts a .asc file to a .mxe cache under maxent.cache/ if not already present; returns the cached path. Skips caching for already-binary formats.
Fills a single sample's env vector by either reading from the LazyGrid or trusting columns already present in the SWD CSV; warns and removes the sample if it has missing data.

nodes.summary: Occurrence record: species name, geographic lon/lat, and pre-extracted env values.
nodes.description: Immutable record carrying the point's row/col in the raster system, lat/lon, species name and an optional HashMap from feature name to Double (used when env values are read from SWD CSV).

methods.description: Computes the row index in dim of this sample's latitude.
Computes the column index in dim of this sample's longitude.

nodes.summary: Container of presence Samples organised by species.
nodes.description: Maps species names to ArrayLists of Samples; offers replication (cross-validation split, bootstrap, subsample), spatial filtering by minimum inter-point distance, duplicate removal at grid-cell or coordinate resolution, and writeback to CSV.

methods.description: Returns all presence points for species s.
Drops samples that share the same grid cell (or same lat/lon when dim is null).
Partitions each species' samples randomly into n folds, mutating this set into the training folds and returning a SampleSet of held-out test folds keyed by species_0…species_n-1.
Creates n bootstrap (with replacement) or copy replicates per species. Used for replicate=>1 runs that are not cross-validation.
Randomly moves a fraction of each species' samples into a returned test SampleSet (destructive split on this set).
Greedy random-order spatial thinning: keeps points at least `mindist` apart.

nodes.summary: SampleSet implementation that reads samples from a CSV (optionally SWD format).
nodes.description: Parses a samples.csv file: species, x, y, plus optional env-value columns. Aligns columns to a given Layer[] so it knows which env-vars are present in the CSV vs which still need grid lookup. Handles the longFirst/latFirst column-order heuristic.

methods.description: Parses every row, skipping species not in the selection set. For each sample, builds the data[] vector, populates speciesMap, and falls back to NODATA_value when an env column is missing.
Returns the env-var column names by reading the header of the sample CSV.
Walks samples and builds each one's featureMap from the data[] vector — needed before training.

nodes.summary: Typesafe accessors for every Maxent parameter, auto-generated from parameters.csv.
nodes.description: Exposes get/set methods for each parameter declared in parameters.csv (responsecurves, jackknife, betamultiplier, hingethreshold, replicates, replicatetype, biasfile, etc). Subclass of ParamsPre.

methods.description: Returns the full list of parameter names.
Global multiplier for automatic regularisation parameters.
Number of replicate runs (for cross-validation / bootstrap / subsample).
Replication strategy: crossvalidate, bootstrap or subsample.
Output transform: cloglog, logistic, cumulative or raw.

nodes.summary: Parses parameters from CSV and from command-line arguments.
nodes.description: Loads parameters.csv into a Parameters table, handles the togglelayertype/togglespeciesselected/togglelayerselected toggles, the bias type aliases (factorbiasout/priordistribution/debiasaverages), and writes the generated Params.java source.

methods.description: Parses argv applying toggles, sets bias type from factorbiasout/priordistribution/debiasaverages.
After toggles have been collected, resolves the final list of selected species, selected layers and layer types.
Parses a single key=value or key directive, including the toggleLayerType / toggleSpeciesSelected variants.

nodes.summary: Single parameter descriptor: name, type, default value, allowed range, tooltip and UI level.
nodes.description: Records the metadata for one row of parameters.csv. Supports types BOOLEAN, INTEGER, DOUBLE, FILE, DIRECTORY, FILEDIRECTORY, STRING (with optional selection list), and UI exposure levels (BASIC, ADVANCED, EXPERIMENTAL, CORE, HIDDEN).

methods.description: Parses the string into the right type with range checks and selection-list validation.
For selection parameters, returns whether s is in the allowed-values list.

nodes.summary: Registry of Parameter objects, supporting flag/abbreviation lookup and argv parsing.
nodes.description: Holds the ordered list of all parameters, plus maps from flag letters and from selection-value strings to their owning Parameter, so the command-line parser can resolve them.

methods.description: Walks argv: short flags like -h, -P become boolean toggles; flag=value sets values; bare selection strings dispatch to the right parameter.
Lookup by name.
Parses a single key/value token.

nodes.summary: Applies a trained .lambdas model to new environmental layers.
nodes.description: Re-instantiates the trained feature set from a .lambdas file (linear, product, quadratic, polyhedral, binary, threshold, hinge, reverse-hinge with their min/max ranges), opens each predictor as a LazyGrid (with ClampedGrid wrappers for the linear bases), and produces an output Grid whose eval(r,c) computes Σⱼ λⱼ · fⱼ(values) − lPN, exponentiates, normalises by densityNormalizer, then applies logistic/cloglog/cumulative transforms.

methods.signature: void doProject(String lambdaFile, String gridDir, String outFile, String clampedFileName)
methods.description: Top-level projection: reads the .lambdas file, opens each input grid (with caching), builds the output Grid, and streams it to disk plus an optional clamp-difference grid.
Returns the prediction grid and the clamping grid (|predClamped − predNoClamp|). For each output pixel, sums λⱼ · fⱼ(env) where fⱼ are reconstructed Pfeature wrappers.

methods.signature: static double interpolateCumulative(double[] raw, double[] cumulative, double t)
methods.description: Maps a raw output t to its cumulative percentile using piecewise-linear interpolation of the raw→cumulative index produced during training.

methods.signature: double logistic(double raw, double entropy, double dp)
methods.description: Logistic transform of the raw model output: dp·v / ((1−dp) + dp·v) where v = raw·exp(entropy).

methods.signature: static double cloglog(double raw, double entropy)
methods.description: Cloglog transform 1 − exp(−raw·exp(entropy)), Maxent's default output format.
Reads the _omission.csv file emitted at training time and returns the [rawValues, cumulativePercentiles] arrays used by interpolateCumulative.

nodes.summary: Programmatic API for scoring a single point with a trained model.
nodes.description: Convenience wrapper around Project: load a .lambdas file once, then call setValue for each variable and evaluate() to get the output at that virtual point. Used by PermutationImportance.

methods.description: Loads the model via Project.projectGrid; the returned Grid is a 1×1 raster whose eval(0,0) reads from the variable map.
Stores a value for the named environmental variable in the shared variable map.
Triggers projectGrid.eval(0,0) which sums λⱼ · fⱼ(varmap).

nodes.summary: Estimates variable importance by permuting one column at a time and measuring AUC drop.
nodes.description: Implements the Breiman 2001 / Strobl 2009 permutation-importance procedure: at each predictor, randomly permutes the column across presence+background, re-scores via Evaluate, and reports the (positive) AUC drop, normalised so they sum to 100.

methods.signature: double[] go(Feature[] features, Sample[] samples, String lambdafile)
methods.description: Builds presence/background value matrices, computes original AUC, then for each variable permutes its column and recomputes AUC; returns the per-variable AUC-drop percentages.

methods.signature: double auc(double[][] a, int index, int[] permutation)
methods.description: Scores all rows of a (presence then background), optionally substituting a permuted column at index, and returns the AUC of presence vs background scores using Stats.auc.

nodes.summary: CLI to compute AUC of a prediction raster against test presences using a random background sample.
nodes.description: Reads test points and a single prediction grid, draws 10000 random background points via Extractor, and reports Stats.auc(presence, background).

methods.description: Reads predfile + testpoints, builds presence and background score arrays, prints AUC.

nodes.summary: Averages a stack of replicate prediction grids and writes avg/stddev/min/max/median/lowerCI rasters.
nodes.description: Used after replicate runs (cross-validation, bootstrap, subsample). Loads each replicate's grid (or projects each .lambdas file via Project) and writes the per-pixel statistics as GridWriter outputs.

methods.signature: void process(String outdir, String species, int n, String gridDir, String outPrefix, String suffix)
methods.description: Loads n replicate models, projects each onto gridDir, and writes the summary stats grids.
√Var(x).
Sorts and returns the middle element.

nodes.summary: Applies a threshold value to a prediction raster, producing a binary GridByte.
nodes.description: Pixels >= threshold become 1, others 0. Returns the predicted-presence fraction.

methods.signature: double applyThreshold(String infile, double threshold, String outfile)
methods.description: Writes the thresholded grid and returns predicted-presence fraction.

nodes.summary: Renders per-variable response curves with Ptolemy MyPlot.
nodes.description: Used by Runner.createProfiles to write a PNG showing how the model output changes as a single variable sweeps its range (with all others held at their sample mean).

methods.signature: void makeplot(double[] x, double[] y, double[] stddev, boolean iscategorical, String var, String title, String ylabel, String outfile, double mmin, double mmax, Params params, boolean exponent, boolean writePlotData)
methods.description: Plots y vs x, optionally with error bars and a thumbnail, and writes the .png plus an optional plot-data CSV.

nodes.summary: Top-level main() — chooses GUI or headless run.
nodes.description: Parses argv into a Params object, optionally pops up the Swing GUI, otherwise invokes Runner.start()/end() in batch mode. Also checks the Java runtime version (≥1.5).

methods.description: Initialises Params from argv. If visible=true, creates the GUI; otherwise calls Runner directly.

nodes.summary: Bulk grid format converter and type-detector.
nodes.description: Walks a directory, detects each grid's actual numeric range, and writes it back in the smallest viable type (byte/ubyte/short/int/float). Also dumps a directory of grids to a single CSV.

methods.description: Reads the grid once via LazyGrid, scans for actual min/max and whether all values are integer, then returns the narrowest typed Grid that can hold them.

methods.signature: static void toCsv(File[] grids, String outdir, String prefix)
methods.description: Writes a row,col,layer1,layer2,… CSV for all grid cells that have data in every layer.

nodes.summary: Applies trained lambdas to NCEAS-format SWD samples and writes per-species score CSVs.
nodes.description: Loads each .lambdas file, recreates the model via FeaturedSpace(.lambdas), then for each sample computes the raw score and writes it (or its logistic/cloglog/cumulative transform).

methods.description: Parses the NCEAS SWD CSV into Sample objects whose featureMap holds each predictor value.

nodes.summary: Sorts indices into a double[] without boxing into Integer[].
nodes.description: Jon Bentley / Doug McIlroy quicksort, ported from the Harpoon project, adapted to sort an int[] of indices by the values of an external double[]. Used by SortedFeatureGenerator and Sequential.featuresToUpdate.

methods.description: Returns indices 0..n-1 sorted so vals[indices[i]] is increasing. Throws on NaN or −0.0.

nodes.summary: Float[] version of DoubleIndexSort.
nodes.description: Same Bentley-McIlroy quicksort but on float[] keys.

methods.description: Returns increasing-key permutation of indices.

nodes.summary: Abstract on-the-fly iterator over n doubles.
nodes.description: Used by FeaturedSpace.getAUC to iterate over background densities without materialising the full array.

methods.description: Returns the next double in the sequence.
Materialises into a normalised double[] for sorting.

nodes.summary: Per-row run-length compression for grid storage.
nodes.description: Encodes runs of repeated values as (count, value) pairs (count 0..127 = repeats, negative = literal run) for short/int/byte/float row arrays, used by Grid.compressedGrid to reduce memory for predictor layers with large no-data regions.

methods.signature: void compress(float[] from, byte[] counts, float[] vals)
methods.description: Encodes from[] into counts[]/vals[] using ±count framing; sets compressionRatio.

methods.signature: void decompress(byte[] counts, float[] vals, float[] into)
methods.description: Inverse of compress.

nodes.summary: Tolerant CSV reader with field-by-name access and an Applier callback.
nodes.description: Detects semicolon separators (French Excel), unquotes quoted fields, lets callers iterate rows via an Applier pattern, build column-indexed maps, and pull whole columns as String[] or double[].

methods.description: Returns the next row's tokens, skipping rows that don't have headers.length fields with a warning.
Iterates over every row, invoking applier.process() — the standard idiom in Runner and others.

methods.signature: static double[] getDoubleCol(String filename, String field)
methods.description: Convenience method to read one numeric column.

nodes.summary: Column-named CSV writer that supports appending and dynamic schemas.
nodes.description: Used by Runner to build maxentResults.csv: callers print into columns by name; columns can be added on-the-fly between rows. Handles re-opening for append and quotes fields that contain commas.

methods.description: Writes a value into the named column of the current row.
Emits the current row, writing the header if this is the first row.

nodes.summary: Project-wide static helpers: logging, progress, fatal error handling, random seed, file utilities.
nodes.description: Holds the global Random generator, the optional MyProgressMonitor, the log PrintStream, the warning-suppression dictionary, and helpers for resolving environmental-layer paths, capitalising strings, writing PrintWriters, and converting between file paths and layer names.

methods.description: Copies a small set of top-level params into static fields (verbose, warnings, visible, NODATA_value, scientificPattern, …).
Updates the progress monitor with x/100 progress and the current progressMsg.

methods.signature: static void fatalException(String s, Throwable e)
methods.description: Logs, pops up an error dialog if visible, and exits.
Workaround for incorrect JVM maxMemory() reports: tries to allocate the target bytes in 1 MB chunks and returns whether it succeeded.

methods.signature: static String[] gridFileNames(String dir, HashSet<String> keep)
methods.description: Enumerates grid files in a directory, filtered to the requested layer-name set, sorted.

nodes.summary: Hand-rolled fast whitespace-delimited token scanner for ASCII grid bodies.
nodes.description: A char-buffered scanner used by GridIO.readASC to avoid the overhead of java.util.Scanner. Supports nextFloat with locale-tolerant comma/dot decimal swap.

methods.description: Returns the next whitespace-delimited token.
Parses the next token as a float, trying comma→dot if NumberFormatException is thrown.

nodes.summary: Fixed-thread-pool task runner used by Runner.jackknifeGain.
nodes.description: Wraps a java.util.concurrent ExecutorService, lets callers add Runnable tasks, runs them all in parallel and propagates exceptions back as fatal errors.

methods.description: Queues a task.
Invokes all queued tasks via invokeAll() and rethrows any execution exception.

nodes.summary: Classic 3rd-generation SwingWorker for off-EDT compute with on-EDT completion.
nodes.description: Used by GUI.doRun so that Runner.start() runs on a worker thread while the Swing event thread stays responsive.

methods.description: Runs on the worker thread.
Invoked on the EDT once construct() returns.
Spawns the worker thread.

nodes.summary: Runtime exception that carries a user-facing message and underlying cause.
nodes.description: Legacy wrapper formerly used to abort Maxent runs with a pop-up message.

nodes.summary: Main Maxent Swing front-end: samples/layers selection, parameter editor, Run button.
nodes.description: Lays out the FileSelect for samples, the DirectorySelect for layers, the per-feature-type checkboxes, the Settings dialog (autogenerated from Parameters at all UI levels), the Help window, and the Run button which spawns a SwingWorker to invoke Runner.

methods.description: Builds the root pane with samples + layers selectors and the central controls panel.
Generates a tabbed Parameters dialog (Basic / Advanced / Experimental / Core) by iterating over Params.allParams().
Reads every editor widget back into the Params object before invoking Runner.
Creates the MyProgressMonitor and spawns a SwingWorker that runs Runner.start().
Reverse of paramsFromInterface — pushes Params values back into the widgets.

nodes.summary: Modeless progress dialog with a Cancel button and a status label.
nodes.description: Wraps a JOptionPane containing a JProgressBar; clicking Cancel fires an ActionListener that calls Runner.stop().

methods.description: Updates the JProgressBar value.
Updates the status label.
Registers the Cancel-button handler.

nodes.summary: Single-line file/directory entry widget with Browse button.
nodes.description: Label + JTextField + Browse button assembly with a JFileChooser and an optional ActionListener fired when the user picks a file.

methods.description: Returns the trimmed entry text.
Sets the entry text and fires the listener.

nodes.summary: Sample-file picker: shows species checkboxes parsed from the CSV's first column.
nodes.description: Subclass of DirectorySelect that, when a samples CSV is chosen, lists each distinct species name with a checkbox so the user can select which to model.

methods.description: Reads the species names from the samples CSV.

nodes.summary: Directory picker that lists checkboxes per environmental layer, with continuous/categorical combos.
nodes.description: Builds a scrollable layer list with select-all/deselect-all buttons. Used in the GUI for both the layers directory and the samples file.

methods.description: Returns names of all checked (or unchecked) layers.
Returns the layer type (Continuous/Categorical) chosen per selected layer.

nodes.summary: Canvas that renders a Grid as a colored heatmap with samples overlaid.
nodes.description: Supports LOG/PLAIN/CLASS color modes, black-and-white, dichromatic, red-and-yellow palettes, sample dots, a legend with auto-positioning, and PNG export. Used by Show and Runner for prediction pictures.

methods.description: Walks all pixels, mapping grid value → color via showColor, drawing samples and the legend.
Writes the current buffered image to a PNG.

nodes.summary: Standalone viewer for grids with zoom, sample overlay and color modes.
nodes.description: JFrame application built on Display, used to inspect .asc/.mxe rasters from the command line or from Maxent's html report links.

methods.description: Constructs the viewer JFrame, the file entry widgets, and the navigation buttons.

nodes.summary: Lightweight predictor + prediction viewer with per-button switching.
nodes.description: Loads a prediction grid plus a directory of predictor grids and lets the user switch the display between them with toolbar buttons.

methods.description: Handles toolbar clicks to switch the displayed grid.

nodes.summary: Generic pannable / zoomable image canvas.
nodes.description: Maintains zoomLevel, center, and an offscreen BufferedImage; handles mouse-drag to sweep a zoom rectangle and click to recenter.

methods.description: Scales the current zoom by r.
Re-renders the image based on current pan/zoom.

nodes.summary: Interactive tool to dissect a Maxent prediction at a clicked point.
nodes.description: Loads a .lambdas model and shows a map alongside per-variable response curves. Clicking the map shows each variable's contribution to the logit (cloglog) at that point as a bar chart.

methods.description: Returns the change in logit when variable i is moved from its sample mean to value v, minus the prorated constant term.
Updates response-curve markers and the per-variable contribution-to-logit bar plot for the clicked pixel.

nodes.summary: Earlier version of Explain that reads precomputed response-curve .dat files.
nodes.description: Predecessor to Explain that reads response curve data from disk instead of recomputing on the fly. Still shipped for backward compatibility.

nodes.summary: JFormattedTextField for editing doubles with up to 9 fractional digits.
nodes.description: Used in the GUI Settings dialog for double-typed parameters.

nodes.summary: JFormattedTextField for editing bounded integers.
nodes.description: Used in the GUI Settings dialog for integer parameters with a min/max range.

nodes.summary: Windows-style INI file reader/writer used to parse .grd headers.
nodes.description: Section + key=value parser ported from Steve DeGroof's public-domain code, made case-insensitive and exception-throwing. Used by GridIO.GRDHeader.

methods.description: Returns the value associated with [subject].variable, case-insensitive.

methods.signature: boolean setValue(String subject, String variable, String value)
methods.description: Sets a value, creating the section if needed.

nodes.summary: CLI: extract environmental values at sample points.
nodes.description: Reads a samples file plus a list of grid files, instantiates an Extractor with maxBackground=0 (no random background), and prints each sample's values across all layers to stdout in CSV.

methods.description: Builds Extractor, prints headers, then for each sample prints lon,lat plus per-layer values (or the NODATA marker).

nodes.summary: CLI: add a constant offset to each non-NODATA cell of one or more grids.
nodes.description: Loops over input grids, builds a wrapping Grid whose eval = orig + offset, and writes to outdir.

methods.signature: void add(double offset, String infile, String outdir)
methods.description: Writes a Grid containing g.eval(r,c)+offset.

nodes.summary: CLI: splits a samples file into train/test using a checkerboard tiling of the study area.
nodes.description: Tiles the bounding box into blocksPerRow × blocksPerCol blocks and routes each sample into the train or test CSV based on the block parity (with edge-exclusion to reduce spatial leakage).

methods.signature: boolean isTrain(double x, double y, GridDimension dim)
methods.description: Returns whether (x,y) lands in a train block.

methods.signature: boolean isExcludedEdge(double x, double y, GridDimension dim)
methods.description: True near a block boundary (excluded for both splits).

nodes.summary: CLI: downsamples grids by summing (2r+1)×(2r+1) windows.
nodes.description: Used to reduce raster resolution while keeping the total mass approximately preserved per coarse cell.

nodes.summary: CLI: prints the Pearson correlation between two grids over pixels where both have data.
nodes.description: Reads both grids fully then calls density.tools.Stats.correlation.

nodes.summary: CLI: counts data cells, sums values, and optionally tabulates value-frequency for grids.
nodes.description: Useful for sanity-checking grid headers and value distributions.

nodes.summary: CLI: rasterises a CSV column into a grid using a template grid's geometry.
nodes.description: Streams rows of the CSV in order, indexing by either row/col or x/y, and writes the chosen column value into the matching pixel.

nodes.summary: Single-pass CSV reader (no rewind), used by tools that stream through huge CSVs.
nodes.description: Simpler/faster than Csv when random column access isn't needed.

nodes.summary: CLI: rasterises (x,y) pairs from a CSV into a 0/1 presence grid using a template grid's dimension.
nodes.description: Marks every grid cell that contains at least one CSV point.

nodes.summary: Experimental: feature that interpolates between f(p) and the sample mean by a bias factor.
nodes.description: eval(p) = sampleMean + bias(p) · (f(p) − sampleMean). Used to factor sampling bias out of a predictor.

nodes.summary: CLI: prints each grid's header (ncols, nrows, xll, yll, cellsize).
nodes.description: Trivial helper for diagnosing grid alignment issues.

nodes.summary: Batch evaluation for the NCEAS benchmark: AUC, correlation, square error, kappa, log-loss per species.
nodes.description: Reads prediction CSVs and ground-truth presence/absence tables and reports a multi-metric table.

methods.description: Pulls presence/absence scores out of map and calls density.tools.Stats.auc.

methods.signature: static double computeLogloss(HashMap map, int which)
methods.description: Negative log-likelihood on presence (which=0), absence (which=1) or combined (which=2).

nodes.summary: CLI: spatially-filters a samples CSV so no two points are within a minimum distance.
nodes.description: Wraps SampleSet.spatialFilter and writes the result to a new CSV.

nodes.summary: CLI: imports BRT-format prediction files back into Maxent's grid format.
nodes.description: Pairs a one-prediction-per-data-cell text file with an index .mxe grid and reconstitutes a Grid.

nodes.summary: CLI: dumps every data cell in a grid directory as tab-separated x,y,val₁,val₂,…
nodes.description: Used to produce SWD-format background tables.

nodes.summary: CLI: builds aligned per-layer histograms across the region, an optional prediction grid, and a samples file.
nodes.description: Outputs a CSV with bin-centre and fractional frequencies for region, prediction, and occurrences.

nodes.summary: CLI: linearly interpolates between two prediction grids over time.
nodes.description: Useful for producing annual slices between two decadal climate projections.

nodes.summary: Computes leave-one-out AUC-like stats from jackknife replicate predictions.
nodes.description: For each held-out point in turn, reads the corresponding species_jN.mxe grid and measures the rank of the held-out point's score relative to all data cells.

nodes.summary: Adds small uniform noise to a SWD CSV (with optional bias-grid weighting).
nodes.description: Used for sensitivity / replicate experiments.

nodes.summary: CLI: produces a grid showing which environmental variable most reduces the prediction at each pixel.
nodes.description: Substitutes each variable in turn with its sample-mean and records which substitution causes the biggest improvement (i.e., which is currently most limiting).

nodes.summary: CLI: masks one grid by another's data-extent.
nodes.description: Returns a Grid whose hasData is the AND of both.

nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic 0–1 'probability of presence' surface that fades at the extremes of each predictor.
nodes.description: Used to construct test datasets with known structure.

nodes.summary: Adds NCEAS dataset/siteid columns to existing prediction outputs.
nodes.description: Bookkeeping helper for the NCEAS species-distribution-modelling benchmark.

nodes.summary: Priority-queue node with row, col, heap index and weight.
nodes.description: Used by NodePriorityQueue inside RestrictToOccupied.

nodes.summary: Min-heap priority queue keyed by Node.qweight, supporting decrease-key.
nodes.description: Used by RestrictToOccupied for Dijkstra-style distance computation.

methods.description: Pushes nd and bubbles up.
Re-heapifies after nd's qweight has been reduced.
Removes and returns the lowest-weight node.

nodes.summary: MESS analysis: highlights regions where projection variables are outside training-data ranges.
nodes.description: Builds per-variable histograms over the training region, then for each projection pixel computes the most novel variable and produces a coloured MoD raster + a continuous MESS raster.

methods.signature: void go(Feature[] basefeatures, String projdir, String outfile)
methods.description: Builds histograms from training features and runs the comparison.

nodes.summary: CLI: sums or averages multiple grids cell-by-cell.
nodes.description: Outputs the mean by default.

nodes.summary: Computes p-median distance, range overlap, midpoint shift and range reduction between presence and pseudo-presence environment sets.
nodes.description: Used in the NCEAS data-quality study to quantify shift between training-only and full sample distributions.

methods.signature: static double pmedianDistance(double[][] a, double[][] b, int measuretype)
methods.description: For each row in a, finds the nearest row in b under the chosen norm (L1, L2, max, geom, err) and returns the median distance.

nodes.summary: Variant of PMedian comparing random background to species data.
nodes.description: Baseline distance measure used to calibrate PMedian.

nodes.summary: CLI: draws nSamples random background points from a stack of grids and prints them as SWD.
nodes.description: Uses Extractor under the hood.

nodes.summary: Draws presence samples from a probability surface, weighted by a bias grid.
nodes.description: Used to simulate biased sampling for synthetic data experiments.

nodes.summary: Uniform random background sampler that requires data in all grids.
nodes.description: Simpler, slower variant of RandomSample for small grid stacks.

nodes.summary: Downsamples a grid by keeping the closest-to-centre data point in each k×k window.
nodes.description: Used to bring a high-resolution grid down to a target pixel count.

nodes.summary: Reprojects a grid by sampling at the centre of each cell of a target dimension.
nodes.description: Nearest-neighbour reproject; useful for aligning grids to a template extent.

nodes.summary: Fades a prediction grid out by distance to the nearest occupied (above-threshold) cell connected to a sample.
nodes.description: Uses a BFS to find the connected component above threshold containing samples, then a Dijkstra over distance to that region, and finally multiplies the prediction by a linearly-decaying weight from mindist to maxdist. Optional convex-hull post-processing.

methods.signature: boolean[][] connected(Grid grid, Sample[] samples, double threshold, int dist)
methods.description: BFS from sample pixels through neighbours with grid.eval ≥ threshold.

methods.signature: double[][] distanceToConn(boolean[][] connected, double maxdist, Grid grid)
methods.description: Dijkstra computing each pixel's geographic distance to the connected component, capped at maxdist.
Computes the convex hull of the occupied region via Graham scan and fills it in.

nodes.summary: Prints 10000 random (val₁, val₂, …) samples from a stack of grids for scatterplotting.
nodes.description: Diagnostic helper.

nodes.summary: Numeric helpers: AUC, Cohen's kappa, mean/variance/stddev, correlation, maxCorrelation.
nodes.description: Hosts the AUC implementation used everywhere (auc() also returns the max-kappa threshold as a side effect) and a stochastic monotonic-rescaling routine that maximises Pearson correlation.

methods.signature: public static double auc(double[] presence, double[] absence)
methods.description: Sorts both arrays and counts ranks, computing AUC as (Σ over pairs of presence-rank-above-absence) / (|presence|·|absence|), and the max-Kappa threshold along the way.

methods.signature: static double correlation(double[] x, double[] y)
methods.description: Pearson correlation between two vectors.

methods.signature: static double maxCorrelation(double[] x, double[] y)
methods.description: Greedy isotonic regression that finds a monotonic transform of x maximising correlation with y.

nodes.summary: Strided downsampling of a directory of grids (keep every k-th pixel).
nodes.description: Preserves the original numeric type unlike Reduce.

nodes.summary: Prints the sum of all data cells in each input grid.
nodes.description: Sanity-check tool.

nodes.summary: Tools version of Threshold: writes a 0/1 grid given a cutoff.
nodes.description: Convenience wrapper similar to density.Threshold but with no NODATA shenanigans.

nodes.summary: Writes a 0/1 grid: 1 wherever the input has data, 0 elsewhere (as NODATA).
nodes.description: Used to derive a mask from any grid's data extent.

nodes.summary: Exports presence + background data into the BRT R-package's CSV format and an index .mxe grid.
nodes.description: Bridges Maxent's SWD pipeline with Elith et al.'s BRT package.

nodes.summary: Writes a 0/1 grid that is 1 where any input grid has data.
nodes.description: Set-union of data extents.

nodes.summary: NCEAS evaluation helpers: reading multi-region eval-file CSVs into presenceMap/absenceMap.
nodes.description: Used by Eval and PMedian.

nodes.summary: Little-endian DataInputStream replacement (Roedy Green, public domain).
nodes.description: Used by LazyGrid and GridIO for reading .gri and .bil binary rasters which are little-endian.

nodes.summary: Little-endian DataOutputStream counterpart.
nodes.description: Used by GridWriter for .gri output.

nodes.summary: GNU getopt port for argv parsing.
nodes.description: Used pervasively by Maxent's CLI tools to parse short flags.
